conversation.text: Three years ago, Cael watched their village of Dawnholt burn. They ran. Everyone knows it. Cael knows it most of all.
Now they stand inside the Tower of Beginnings, cold stone beneath their boots, a spectral figure waiting in the dark ahead.
You came. Most who carry guilt this heavy find reasons not to.
I didn't come for redemption. I came because the dreams wouldn't stop.
The Tower calls through dreams when it has decided. You don't get to choose whether you were chosen.
What does it want from me?
Not from you. Of you. There is a difference.
The Tower has seven trials. Each one will reach into you and find something you buried. The first trial is the simplest: survival.
And if I fail?
You begin again. The Tower is patient. It has been waiting for someone like you for a very long time. It can afford to wait a little longer.
Someone like me. A coward.
Someone who survived. The Tower does not distinguish between the two yet. That is what the trials are for.
A mark burns itself onto the back of Cael's left hand. A small flame, barely the size of a coin. It does not hurt. That somehow makes it worse.
Above, the sky opens. A bird of pure wind descends, its feathers made of air and motion. The Trial of the Sky Bridge begins.

scenes.title: Trial One: The Sky Bridge
content: The spectral bird carries you out over a chasm of endless wind.
Below, the ruins of a city swallowed by clouds drift in and out of view.
The Keeper's words echo: the Tower does not demand perfection. Only persistence.
You grip the bird's mane. You have run from things before.
Not this time.

scenes.victory_message: You crossed the Sky Bridge. The flame on your hand burns a little brighter.
scenes.defeat_message: The wind takes you. The Tower resets. The flame dims, but does not go out.

conversation.text: Cael lands hard on the far side, gasping. The soul-mark on their hand pulses once, twice, then settles into a slow steady glow.
The mark tallied your falls. You crossed. That is what matters.
What comes next?
The Mines of Memory. This trial does not test your body. It tests what you do when the past reaches up through the ground and grabs your ankle.
Whose memories are down there?
Everyone who failed a trial they weren't ready for. Their pain crystallised into the stone. You will feel it with every wrong step.
That's cruel.
It is honest. Most cruelty is.

scenes.title: Trial Two: The Mines of Memory
content: The mines breathe.
Not wind — something slower. Like the exhalation of something ancient
that has been holding its breath for centuries.
The glowing shards beneath your feet are not stones.
They are moments. Frozen grief. Someone's worst day, crystallised.
Step carefully.
Every wrong move detonates someone else's sorrow directly into your chest.

conversation.text: The last shard detonates before Cael can stop it. A wave of foreign grief crashes through them: the memory of a soldier named Mira, who also ran from a burning village, who also carried her guilt to the Tower, who did not make it past the third trial.
You felt that, didn't you. The shame tastes the same in everyone's mouth.
Who are you?
Someone who came before you. I left an echo in the mines when I failed. The Tower keeps us here as warnings. Or lessons. Depending on your perspective.
What happened to you? At the third trial?
There is a guardian ahead who wears your face. She wore mine. I saw myself and I froze. Don't freeze. Whatever she says to you — she is not wrong, but she is not the whole truth either.
Why are you helping me?
Because if you make it through, some part of what I carried will have meant something. Now go. The rune gate is waiting.
The soul-mark on Cael's hand shifts. The small flame now burns with a faint silver thread woven through it. Mira's mark, folded into Cael's own.

scenes.title: The Rune Gate Hall
content: The gate is older than the Tower itself.
The runes carved into it are not a language — they are a question,
asked in a script that predates words.
The Keeper told you nothing about this one.
Some puzzles you have to solve alone.
You study the tiles. You think of Mira.
You begin.

conversation.text: The gate opens. Beyond it: a corridor of polished black stone that reflects Cael back at themselves from a dozen angles.
You solved it without asking for help.
I had help. Just not from anyone living.
The echo in the mines. You listened to her.
She warned me about the guardian ahead. The one that wears my face.
The Mirror Guardian reflects the challenger's deepest doubt back at them. For you, that doubt has a name.
Dawnholt.
The trial is not about punishing you for what you did. It is about finding out if you have decided who you are now.
The corridor ends at a vault door. A single word is required. Cael stares at the keyhole and thinks about Dawnholt. About the thing they never let themselves say out loud.

scenes.title: The Forgotten Vault
content: The vault door has no lock you can pick.
It has a word.
The Keeper said: the Tower reaches into you and finds something you buried.
You buried one word deeper than all the others.
The word you never said after Dawnholt.
The word that would have made it real.
You know what it is.
You have always known.

clues: It is the word you have not said since Dawnholt.
Five letters. It begins where guilt ends.
Not an excuse. Not an explanation.
Just the truth, offered up with nothing held back.

conversation.text: The vault opens. Inside: not treasure. Not weapons. Just a room full of light, and in the centre, a stone basin containing a single, still flame.
Most people guess wrong for hours. Courage. Strength. Honor. They never think it could be something so small.
It doesn't feel small.
No. It never does. That's why you buried it.
The Arena of Reflection is ahead. The Mirror Guardian has been watching since you entered the mines. She knows what you just did. She will use it.
Good. Let her.
The soul-mark burns suddenly, fully, like a torch catching. The flame on Cael's hand is no longer small. It is the size of a fist and it casts real shadows.

scenes.title: Trial Four: The Arena of Reflection
content: The arena is circular. Every wall is a mirror.
In every mirror: you.
Not the you that is standing here now.
The you from three years ago, frozen in the moment of running.
Seventeen years old. Terrified. Choosing survival over everything else.
The Mirror Guardian steps out from the largest mirror.
She has your face.
She has Mira's eyes.

conversation.text: You said sorry. Very moving. Does that bring anyone back?
No.
Then what was the point? You opened a vault door. You feel better about yourself. The people of Dawnholt are still dead.
I know.
Is that all you have to say? I know?
What do you want me to say? That I should have died with them? Maybe. But I didn't. And I'm here now, and I'm not running.
You're very brave inside a Tower that won't let you actually die. Let's see how brave you are when something real is at stake.
The mirrors shatter. In their place: a battlefield, perfectly square, perfectly still. The Mirror Guardian raises her hand. The duel begins.

scenes.victory_message: The Guardian lowers her hand. She looks almost surprised. Almost proud.
scenes.defeat_message: The Guardian steps back. 'Again,' she says. Not unkindly.

conversation.text: You held the line.
I had a good teacher. She's been dead for a while, but still.
Mira's thread is in your mark now. You didn't just learn from her memory. You became a continuation of it.
She deserved to make it through herself.
Yes. She did. Most of the good ones do. That is why the Tower keeps trying.
The Mirror Guardian steps back into the largest mirror. For a moment, before she disappears, she looks like Mira. Then she is gone.

scenes.title: Trial Five: The Unravelling
content: The Tower's next trial arrives without ceremony.
A room full of falling pieces.
A ceiling that descends if you stop moving.
What this room finds in you is the part that panics when things fall apart.
The part that goes still at exactly the wrong moment.
Don't go still.
Keep building.
Even when the shapes are wrong.
Even when there is no good move.
Especially then.

scenes.victory_message: The ceiling stops. The room holds. So do you.
scenes.defeat_message: The pieces overwhelm you. The room resets. The ceiling rises. Try again.

conversation.text: The final chamber is not what Cael expected. No grand hall. No throne. Just a circular room, and in its centre, the Keeper of Beginnings, sitting on the floor like someone who has been waiting for a very long time.
Six trials. You have one left.
What is it?
A choice. But first you should know what you are choosing between.
The Silence has been spreading for forty years. It does not kill. It erases. Villages, forests, memories: gone as though they never existed. Dawnholt was one of its first incursions.
Dawnholt wasn't a fire. It was the Silence.
The fire was how it arrived that time. It wears different faces.
I ran from something that erases things. And I've been blaming myself for three years.
You were seventeen and you survived. That was not a moral failure. It was the point. The Tower needed you to survive.
You could have just told me that at the beginning.
Would you have believed it?
A long silence. The soul-mark on Cael's hand burns steadily, silver and gold, Mira's thread braided through their own.
No. I wouldn't have.
The final trial is a door. Two paths. One leads out of the Tower and back to your life. The other leads toward the Silence.
I have been the Keeper for two hundred years. I have watched four hundred and twelve people reach this room. You are the seventh to make it this far.
What happened to the other six?
They chose the door toward the Silence. They are still out there. Fighting. They became the only thing that stands between the world and what is coming. They were not lost. They became beacons.
Is one of them Mira?
No. Mira did not reach this room. But her mark is in your hand, and your hand is here. That is the closest she will ever come.
Two doors appear in the far wall. One glows warm: the world, unchanged, waiting. One is dark: the unknown, and something beyond it that needs to be faced.

scenes.title: Trial Six: The Choice
content: Two guards stand before two doors.
One tells only truth. One tells only lies.
You may ask one question.
But you already know, don't you.
You knew the moment the Keeper said Dawnholt was the Silence.
You knew the moment you saw Mira's thread in your mark.
The riddle is not which door leads out.
The riddle is whether you are ready to stop running.

scenes.victory_message: You chose the dark door. Behind you, the warm door seals. Ahead, the Silence waits. So do six beacons.
scenes.defeat_message: The choice unmakes you for a moment. The guards wait. They are patient. Try again.

scenes.title: Trial Seven: The Long Dark
content: One last trial before you leave the Tower.
The Silence moves like a living thing.
It does not fight. It consumes.
It learns the shape of you and finds the gaps.
You have faced yourself.
You have faced your past.
Now face what is coming.
Don't let it find the gaps.

scenes.victory_message: The darkness finds no gaps. You are whole. The Tower's doors open.
scenes.defeat_message: The darkness finds a gap. You seal it. You try again. The Tower waits.

conversation.text: The dark door opens. Beyond it is not darkness at all: it is the world, seen from above, vast and scarred and worth saving.
I have to go now.
Already?
My purpose was to bring someone to this room. I have done that. The Tower will have a new Keeper now.
I don't know how to fight the Silence.
You know how to get up after falling. You know how to say the word you buried. You know how to hold the line when everything is coming at you at once. That is more than enough to begin.
The Keeper dissolves. Not like death: like a held breath finally released.
The other six know you are coming. The Silence moves west, toward the coast. You have perhaps three days.
One last thing. Mira's echo in the mines. Is she still down there?
No. When you carried her lesson through to the end, her echo was released. She is not in the mines anymore. She is in the mark. She goes where you go.
Good.

Cael walks through the dark door. Below, the world waits. The soul-mark casts real light now, silver and gold, steady as a heartbeat. Somewhere to the west, six other lights are moving toward the same horizon.

scenes.title: Credits
content: Thank you for playing Tower of Beginnings.
Story by: bellatrixesque
Game Design: bellatrixesque
Art and Assets: Sora
Programming: Claude
In memory of all the Miras.
The ones who didn't make it through,
but made it possible for someone else to.
